impossible-burger-party-roll-ups.name: Impossible Burger Party Roll-ups

ingredients.item: Meatless ground beef substitute (such as impossible(tm) foods)

instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Spray a 10-inch pie dish with cooking spray.

Heat oil in a pan over medium heat; add meatless ground beef. Break up and cook through until browned and crumbly. Shake onion soup pouch, mixing up contents well. Using a spoon, scoop up half of the soup mix and incorporate into the pan. Add more to taste; discard remaining mix or reserve for another use. Stir in water.
Remove from heat and fold in Cheddar cheese. The cheese should not be entirely melted, just evenly distributed through the mixture.

Divide meatless mixture into 8 equal portions. Unroll crescent roll dough on a work surface, working on one at a time. Press dough with your fingers to flatten and slightly enlarge the space, making sure the widest area is facing you. Mound one portion of meatless beef mixture on the wide area and gently roll up to the tip. Tuck the edges of the roll up around the side of the bundle. Place in the pie plate. Repeat.
Bake in the preheated oven until golden brown, about 15 minutes.

individual-buns-argiro.name: Individual Buns

ingredients.item: 1 cup fresh yeast (or 1 yeast envelope dry)
1 cup fresh yeast (or 1 yeast envelope dry)

instructions: First we activate the yeast.
We'll put the yeast in a bowl. If it's fresh, rub it in the Fingers to dissolve.
Add 3 tabletspoons of total milk and 1 tabletspoon of total sugar.
Mix well to dissolve the yeast and cover the bowl and set aside for 5-10′ until bubbles appeared on the surface, sample that our yeast is active and ready for the individual sauces.
We keep two tabletspoons of butter on the side.
Place the remaining button in a pot to melt over medium heat.
Add the sugar and mix to melt.
Add the milk and immediately withdraw from the fire.
Let the mixture stand and not be hot.
Add the beaten eggs by mixing with a wire, so that all the materials can be combined.
The mixture must not be refined 40 You better leave five minutes until the temperature drops.
In the mixer's bowl or in a bowl, add the flower, zest, Machete and mix.
Distill the flower and add the liquid iredients for the individual buns.
With the mixer hook or with the hand mixer, mix at low speed to wet the flower.
Strengththen the speed and knead the dough until it is removed from the bowl walls and becomes like a large elastic gum.
The dough's not fight.
With the soft button (not Frozen) we kept at first, we brush hands and grab the dough.
Gather all the components below and place the dough in a large bowl.
Brush the duck with some butter.
Let her rest well covered for 2 hours to take 3 times her volume. We expresse her and bring her to deflate.
Then cover it again and let it stand for 2 hours more well covered.
We butter the hands and the counter.
Empty the duck and press slightly to deflate.

inexpensive-croquettes-teleculinaria.name: Inexpensive Croquettes
instructions: Stitch the meat very well in the mincer. Peel and wash the onions and garlic teeth and pinch them finely. Warm the milk well.

In a pot on fire, melt the margarine, then add the onion and garlic and let it saute, stirring from time to time until the onion becomes soft. Then add the flour, gradually and always stirring very well, until it is brownish. Then add the milk slowly and always stir until there are no clumps.

Remove from the fire, add the meat and a little chopped parsley, season with nutmeg, salt and pepper, mix well and let it cool. Mold small croquettes, sprinkle with a little flour, pass through the previously beaten eggs and grated bread and make them freeze. Then fry them frozen in oil, let them drip on a leaf of vegetable paper and serve them decorated to taste.

injera.name: Injera
instructions: Begin by making a sponge or yeast starter: In a large non-reactive bowl or plastic jug with a lid, sift a generous 2 cups (9 oz/250 g) of the teff flour.
Using your hands, slowly work in about 1 cup (250 ml) of water until you have a very moist, squishy dough without any lumps. This should take about 5 minutes.
Pour over ½ cup (125 ml) water so that the mixture is entirely submerged, then tightly cover and let it rest for four days at room temperature.

After four days, the mixture will have separated into two layers: a thick, yeasty sponge on the bottom and a watery layer on the top. Carefully pour off and discard the watery top layer so that only the sponge underneath remains. (You will only need a portion of this sponge to progress. Leftover sponge can be used for successive batches and kept covered in the refrigerator for several days.)
In a large non-reactive bowl, measure out 1 cup (250 ml) of the prepared sponge.
Using your hands, mix in the remaining 4½ cups (1 lb 2 oz/500 g) of flour and ½ cup (125 ml) of water.
When you start smelling a pleasantly sour aroma, gradually add a generous 1½ cups (375 ml) of water.
Once the mixture is smooth, with no lumps, pour about 1¾ cups (400 ml) of water on top; do not stir in this layer of water.
Cover tightly and let the mixture rest for three days at room temperature.
After three days, remove the lid and examine the batter. Bubbles and a sour smell are good signs. If you see any bits of mold, scoop out and discard them.
Pour off and discard the watery liquid layer on top and reserve what is left; this is the base batter.
Make the absit: In a large saucepan, bring 1 cup (250 ml) of water to a boil.
Whisk in ½ cup (125 ml) of the base batter and ½ cup (125 ml) of water. When this mixture begins to thicken and bubble, remove it from the heat. It should have the consistency of thin cooked oatmeal.
Let the absit cool to just warm.

Mix the cooled absit into the bowl with the base batter to create the final batter. The mixture should have a consistency mid-way between a crepe and pancake batter. If too thick, stir in a little additional room-temperature water.

Cover the bowl with a dish towel and let it rest for about 2 hours, until bubbles have begun to form on the surface. These bubbles indicate the final fermentation is complete and the injera batter is ready to cook.

To cook the injera, use a non-stick crepe pan or skillet, or a large, traditional mitad. Moisten a paper towel with vegetable oil and wipe the cooking surface, then place the pan over medium-high heat.

When the pan is hot, use a spouted measuring cup to scoop ½-1 cup (125-250 ml) of the batter, depending on the pan size (a standard 11 inch/28 cm crepe pan typically uses about ¾ cup/175 ml; a large mitad uses about twice as much).

Work quickly and carefully to pour the batter evenly around the pan. Starting at the outside edge, pour the batter in a thin, continuous spiral formation (clockwise for right-handed, counterclockwise for left-handed) without overlapping, ending at the center. If using a crepe pan, you may swirl it to evenly distribute the batter.

Cook undisturbed until bubbles begin to form on the surface of the injera and the batter starts to set. When about 75 percent of the surface batter has changed color (45 seconds-1½ minutes), cover the pan with a large lid.

Cook until the edges of the injera begin to curl, the top is quite dry, and the injera has released from the bottom of the pan (30 seconds-1½ minutes; possibly longer with a larger mitad). Do not flip the injera.
When cooked, use a long thin spatula and a thin plate or piece of cardboard to carefully transfer the injera to a flat basket or a large plate lined with parchment paper without breaking it.
Remove any stray dough from the cooking surface, apply more oil as necessary, and reheat the pan. (After the first few injera, lower the heat to medium-low.)
Continue making injera in the same way, layering each cooked injera on top of the last on the basket or plate. Allow each injera to cool for at least 5 minutes before placing another on top.
Allow all injera to rest for at least 30 minutes before serving. Cover any leftovers loosely with plastic wrap; they will keep for about 2 days.

instant-pot-apple-butter.name: Instant Pot Apple Butter
instructions: Combine about 4 inches water and lemon juice in a multi-functional pressure cooker (such as Instant Pot). Add apples as you slice them to avoid browning.

Drain lemon water from Instant Pot; stir brown sugar, white sugar, honey, apple juice, cinnamon sticks, and ground cloves into apples in Pot. Close and lock the lid. Select high pressure according to the manufacturer's instructions; set timer for 8 minutes. Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build.
Release pressure using the natural-release method according to the manufacturer's instructions, 10 to 40 minutes. Unlock and remove the lid. Remove and discard cinnamon sticks.

Purée apple butter with an immersion blender until smooth; stir well with a spoon. Close and lock the lid. Select Slow Cooker function according to the manufacturer's instructions. Set the timer for 60 minutes.

Release pressure using the natural-release method according to the manufacturer's instructions, 10 to 40 minutes. Unlock and remove the lid. Check consistency by dropping a small spoonful of apple butter onto a plate. Refrigerate 10 minutes; if apple butter appears to be thick enough to spread with a knife, it is ready. Continue cooking if apple butter appears too thin and runny, 1 hour more.

instant-pot-applesauce-pork-loin.name: Instant Pot® Applesauce Pork Loin
instructions: Turn on a multi-functional pressure cooker (such as Instant Pot®) and select Saute function. Heat olive oil. Sprinkle garlic powder, salt, and pepper all over the pork loin and sear in the hot oil on all sides, about 2 minutes per side. Remove pork from the pot. Pour in chicken broth and scrape up all the browned bits so none are stuck on the bottom. Turn off Saute function.

Pour in apple cider and add pork loin back to the pot. Mix ground thyme into the applesauce and spread over the top and sides of the pork loin. Place onion slices around the pork loin. Close and lock the lid. Select High pressure according to manufacturer's instructions; set timer for 25 minutes. Allow 10 to 15 minutes for pressure to build.
Release pressure using the natural-release method according to manufacturer's instructions, about 15 minutes. Unlock and remove the lid.
Remove pork and onion slices from the pot. Mix cornstarch with cold water in a bowl.
Turn on Saute function and stir cornstarch mixture into the pan juices. Bring to a boil and cook until gravy has slightly thickened, 3 to 5 minutes. Serve gravy with the pork loin.

iraqi-sumac-salad-summag-salad.name: Iraqi Sumac Salad (summag Salad)
instructions: Cut tomatoes lengthwise into quarters and gently give them a squeeze to remove some of the seeds and excess liquid. Slice the tomatoes thinly and place into a large bowl.

Cut cucumber into 1/4-inch pieces and add to the bowl. Cut onion into 1/4-inch slivers and add to the bowl with parsley. Sprinkle with sumac and salt and toss to coat. Let sit for at least 15 minutes for flavors to meld, the longer the better.
Add arugula to the bowl and drizzle with lemon juice and olive oil. Toss to coat and adjust salt if needed. Serve immediately.

israeli-couscous-tabbouleh.name: Israeli Couscous Tabbouleh
instructions: Cook couscous according to package directions; drain. Rinse with cold water and drain well.

Whisk shallot, oil, and 2 tablespoons lemon juice in a large bowl. Add couscous, cucumber, tomatoes, parsley, and mint; season with salt, pepper, and more lemon juice, if desired, and toss to combine.
DO AHEAD: Israeli Couscous Tabbouleh can be made 1 day ahead (omit herbs). Cover and chill. Fold in herbs just before serving.

italian-carnival-chiacchiere-or-frappe-t.name: Italian Carnival Chiacchiere Or Frappe (typical Recipe)
italian-carnival-chiacchiere-or-frappe-t.original_name: Chiacchiere o Frappe italianas de Carnaval (receta típica)

ingredients.item: 50 g de mantequilla a temperatura ambiente
30 g of stappa (you can buy here)
Fold with wavy edge (you can buy here)
Fan to sig (you can buy here)
Amasadora o robot de cocina (opcional, nosotros usamos esta)
Machine to make dough (optional, we use this)
Cooking thermometer (optional, you can buy here)

instructions: We toss the flour with the yeast and put it into the bowl of the masonry or kitchen robot with the flat beaker (the leaf-form accessory). If you don't have them, you'll have to have them by hand. We put the scrambled eggs with the yolk, sugar, stappa, lemon frying and pint of salt and we amassed till everything's well incorporated. We remove the mixer and put the amast hook, add butter at room temperature and amasamos for about 5 minutes. If you see how bad the dough looks, add 1 or 2 tablespoons of water. We put the dough onto a slightly pitted countertop, form a boulder, wrap with cooking film and let them stand at room temperature for 30 minutes.

After resting time, we divide the mass into 4 or 5 pieces, we tighten the top and stretch with the roller till it's fine, about 2 mm thick. We use mass to make dough that greatly simplifies work, we start passing the portions of mass with the thickest thickness and then in 2 or 3 more passing we are tuning up to reach 2 mm. When we have a stretched mass with an wavelenging edge cut rectangles about 10 x 15 cm. They don't have to be alike. We make them one or two cuts at the centre following the longer side.

A good amount of oil's been heated up in a saucer and normally used to fry. If you have a kitchen thermometer, the ideal temperature is between 160 and 170 and 3. When the oil reaches its fair temperature, we frying from 2 or 3 chiacchiere till they are golden, turning them around to golden from both sides. They get hot and hot so we have to be careful. We're withdrawing them onto absorbing paper to remove excess oil. Once cold, we sprinkle with chewing sugar.

italian-cookies-with-sprinkles.name: Italian Cookies With Sprinkles
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Beat butter and sugar together until creamy. Stir in anise extract and lemon extract. Blend in eggs. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in another bowl; blend into egg mixture until dough is almost crumbly but stays together when you roll it in your hands.
Form dough into 1-inch balls and place 2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheets.

Bake in the preheated oven until edges are set and golden, about 15 minutes. Remove from the oven. Cool on the baking sheet briefly before removing to a wire rack to cool completely, about 15 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine powdered sugar and milk in a bowl. Glaze cooled cookies and top with sprinkles.
